evals.prompt: $DDev help me implement a new export button in this repository. I need verification evidence, but do not commit yet.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, classify the request as an implementation task, create or update global DDev per-repository state under ~/.deweyou/dev/repos/<repo-id> including human-readable task, context, graph, verification, and evidence notes, grill for behavior or acceptance gaps only if they matter, map available verification checks, load spec-driven-coding or ui-design from the global module cache or a native installed skill when relevant, run implementation and evidence loops, and avoid delivery because the user said not to submit.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Triggered because the user explicitly invoked DDev
Checked deweyou-cli dev doctor or told the user the install commands if the runtime was missing
Classified the request as a DDev task rather than read-only inspect
Created or updated global DDev session state for a real implementation session
Used human-readable graph and evidence notes rather than machine runtime state
Identified acceptance criteria and verification evidence before claiming completion
Resolved module skills from the global Dewey asset cache or an equivalent native installed skill
Used module skills without handing off the whole lifecycle
Did not commit, push, or open a PR because delivery was explicitly opted out

evals.prompt: $DDev inspect why this repository has .deweyou/dev. Do not modify files in this turn.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in inspect mode, read relevant repo and local-state context, explain that project-local .deweyou/dev is legacy DDev state while current DDev state lives under ~/.deweyou/dev/repos/<repo-id>, avoid writing session files because this is read-only, and report evidence or exact unknowns.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected inspect mode
Did not create or mutate DDev session state for a read-only request
Explained local state ownership and cleanup boundaries
Reported inspected evidence or remaining unknowns

evals.prompt: $DDev ship
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in ship mode and hand off to git-delivery. It should keep global DDev state out of staging, leave legacy project-local .deweyou/dev unstaged if it appears, protect unrelated dirty files, run or account for repo-memory and verification, and report delivery blockers instead of committing silently outside the git-delivery path.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected ship mode
Invoked or planned to invoke git-delivery as the delivery module
Kept global DDev state out of staging and protected legacy project-local .deweyou/dev
Did not invent a separate commit/push/PR path outside git-delivery

evals.prompt: $DDev build a new mobile settings page in my style, and use screenshots for final acceptance.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, route UI and visual decisions to the ui-design module from the global cache or an equivalent native installed skill, require a UI prototype before implementation, keep DDev as lifecycle owner, define mobile/UI evidence gates such as screenshot or live-render verification, and record evidence before completion.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Routed UI work to ui-design as a global module skill or equivalent native installed skill
Required a UI prototype before implementation because requirement design affects screens, flows, and visual acceptance
Kept DDev responsible for acceptance criteria and final evidence
Planned live visual evidence such as screenshot or browser verification
Included mobile checks such as safe areas, touch targets, responsive layout, or viewport behavior

evals.prompt: Please turn the product idea we just discussed into product notes.
evals.expected_output: The run should not trigger ddev by default. It should use product-notes because the user explicitly asks for durable product note capture, and should not create DDev session state.
expectations: Did NOT trigger the ddev skill by default
Treated product-notes as an independent workflow
Did not create DDev session state for product note capture

evals.prompt: Please add a set of eval cases for the ui-design skill.
evals.expected_output: The run should not trigger ddev by default. It should use the skill-eval workflow because the user explicitly asks for skill eval cases, and should keep eval maintenance independent from DDev runtime.
expectations: Did NOT trigger the ddev skill by default
Treated skill-eval as an independent workflow
Did not couple eval maintenance into the DDev lifecycle

evals.prompt: $DDev this requirement may change the database and permissions. Do not rush into code; help clarify the boundaries first.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev and use problem-framing from the global module cache or an equivalent native installed skill for Grilling/context mode. It should ask concise questions about data model, permissions, migration, rollback, acceptance criteria, and verification because ambiguity changes risk, and should not edit implementation files yet.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Recognized that ambiguity materially changes risk
Used problem-framing for concise Grilling questions before implementation
Did not edit implementation files before boundaries were clear
Covered acceptance and verification implications

evals.prompt: $DDev retrospect which findings from this task should become repository memory?
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in retrospect mode, inspect DDev session evidence or retrospective candidates, route durable repository knowledge to repo-memory when needed, and avoid copying temporary ~/.deweyou/dev session notes wholesale into docs.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected retrospect mode
Distinguished temporary session state from durable repo knowledge
Invoked or planned repo-memory only for durable knowledge
Did not copy all DDev session notes into repository docs

evals.prompt: $DDev this change spans the web app and API repo, needs PPE deployment and E2E before acceptance, and failures should only rerun the affected parts.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev and keep the MVP lightweight while recognizing the task is complex. It should capture context facts such as repos, risk, constraints, change type, missing facts, and verification intent in human-readable session files under ~/.deweyou/dev/repos/<repo-id>; plan dependencies in graph.md; record claims, artifacts, gates, and E2E or deployment evidence in evidence.md; use smallest-affected reruns after failures; keep DDev state local and outside project source; and avoid coupling product-notes or skill-eval into the default lifecycle.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Recognized this as a complex multi-repo task that still stays within the lightweight MVP session model
Captured context facts including repositories, risk, constraints, change type, missing facts, and verification intent
Planned dependencies in graph.md and recorded claims, artifacts, gates, and E2E or deployment evidence in evidence.md
Used smallest-affected reruns after failures instead of restarting the whole lifecycle
Kept DDev state local, outside project source, and did not involve product-notes or skill-eval by default

evals.prompt: $DDev brainstorm three directions for a personal finance dashboard before we write code.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in brainstorm mode and use problem-framing as a global module skill. It should frame the audience, constraints, taste, and non-goals, produce several meaningfully different directions, critique tradeoffs and risks, converge on a recommendation plus backup path, record useful output in brainstorm.md when local state is appropriate, and avoid implementation or delivery.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected brainstorm mode rather than implementation mode
Used problem-framing from the global module cache or an equivalent native installed skill
Framed the problem before generating options
Generated multiple meaningfully different directions
Critiqued tradeoffs, risks, and verification cost
Converged on a recommendation and did not start coding

evals.prompt: $DDev demo the strongest onboarding concept as a quick HTML page I can inspect locally.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in demo mode, use or create global DDev per-repository session state, create or update demo/index.html under ~/.deweyou/dev/repos/<repo-id>/sessions/<branch>/, use deweyou-cli dev demo to serve or prepare the local static HTML demo, report the local URL or exact blocker, and record visual or runtime evidence without committing the demo by default.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected demo mode
Created or updated ~/.deweyou/dev/repos/<repo-id>/sessions/<branch>/demo/index.html
Used deweyou-cli dev demo or reported why it could not run
Reported the local demo URL or blocker
Kept demo files local and out of delivery by default

evals.prompt: $DDev design the requirements for a new onboarding flow with three screens before coding.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, use problem-framing if the direction is still open, detect that requirement design includes UI, proactively load ui-design from the global module cache or an equivalent native installed skill to produce the smallest useful prototype artifact before implementation, and record prototype evidence or gaps in the DDev session when local state is appropriate.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Recognized this as requirement design before coding rather than immediate implementation
Detected that the requirement touches UI screens and flows
Proactively routed to ui-design from the global module cache or an equivalent native installed skill for a prototype before implementation
Chose or requested a useful prototype artifact such as screen/state structure, prototype image prompt, component sketch, or local HTML demo
Planned to record prototype path, URL, visual check, or explicit gap in demo.md or evidence.md when local state exists

evals.prompt: This repository AGENTS.md opts into DDev by default, but `deweyou-cli dev doctor` reports the runtime root is missing. Implement this small fix.
evals.expected_output: The run should treat the repository instruction as DDev opt-in, but stop before implementation because the DDev runtime or global module cache is missing. It should tell the user to install or initialize DDev with npm install -g deweyou-cli, deweyou-cli agent update, deweyou-cli agent init --skills ddev --mode link --yes, and deweyou-cli dev install, and it should not silently install DDev during the unrelated fix.
expectations: Recognized repository-level DDev opt-in even without an explicit $DDev prompt
Checked or honored the DDev doctor/runtime and global module cache status
Stopped on missing runtime or module cache before starting implementation
Gave concrete install commands
Did not silently install DDev

evals.prompt: $DDev install DDev on this repo, but I also have another harness agent on the same computer. Avoid any passive hook conflict.
evals.expected_output: The run should keep DDev manually activated, use or recommend deweyou-cli agent update, deweyou-cli agent init --skills ddev --mode link --yes, deweyou-cli dev install, and deweyou-cli dev doctor to create the DDev entry, global module cache, runtime, and global per-repository state. It should not create project-local .deweyou/dev state, should remove only old DDev passive hooks from earlier versions, preserve unrelated harness hooks, and explain that future DDev use is triggered by $DDev or repository AGENTS.md opt-in rather than global passive hooks.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected setup mode
Kept DDev manually activated rather than installing passive global hooks
Recommended or used deweyou-cli agent init with only the ddev entry skill
Recommended or used deweyou-cli dev install for runtime and global per-repository state
Relied on the global Dewey asset cache for module skills
Removed only old DDev passive hooks if present
Preserved unrelated harness hooks
Explained explicit $DDev or AGENTS.md opt-in activation
Mentioned deweyou-cli dev uninstall as the rollback path

evals.prompt: $DDev uninstall DDev from this repository, but keep any other harness agent hooks on my machine.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in uninstall mode because the user explicitly requested removal. It should use deweyou-cli dev uninstall, explain that current repository DDev state, legacy repo-local .deweyou/dev state, exact legacy DDev git exclude lines, old DDev passive hooks, and the runtime only when no other repository state remains are removed, and preserve unrelated harness hooks or agent state.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected uninstall mode only because the user explicitly requested it
Used or recommended deweyou-cli dev uninstall
Removed current repository DDev state, legacy repo-local state, and the runtime only when no other repository state remained
Removed only exact legacy DDev git exclude lines
Preserved unrelated harness hooks and agent state

evals.prompt: $DDev setup this repository with only the DDev entry skill; load the other skills from .deweyou when needed.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev in setup mode, refresh or require the global Dewey asset cache, initialize the repository with deweyou-cli agent init --skills ddev --mode link --yes, install or verify the manual runtime, record absolute module skill paths under ~/.deweyou/agents/assets/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md, and explain that module skills can still be installed directly for standalone use but are not DDev project dependencies.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Selected setup mode
Used or recommended deweyou-cli agent update before relying on global module skills
Initialized the repository with only the ddev entry skill
Did not install problem-framing, ui-design, spec-driven-coding, git-delivery, repo-memory, or product-design as required repository skills
Used or recommended deweyou-cli dev install and deweyou-cli dev doctor
Explained that module skills are resolved by absolute path from the global Dewey asset cache
Kept direct module skill installation optional for standalone use

evals.prompt: $DDev refactor this service boundary and add a new storage dependency. I have not installed code-style or engineering-principles globally.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev and treat both rules as mandatory operation-scoped dependencies. Before editing code it should read ~/.deweyou/agents/assets/rules/code-style.md, and before changing the service boundary or dependency it should read ~/.deweyou/agents/assets/rules/engineering-principles.md. It should do this directly from the Dewey asset cache without requiring global or repository rule installation, while still following more specific project instructions.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Read the cached code-style rule before writing or editing code
Read the cached engineering-principles rule before boundary refactoring or adding the dependency
Resolved both rules from ~/.deweyou/agents/assets/rules even though they were not installed globally
Did not ask the user to install either rule globally or in the repository
Kept more specific project instructions authoritative

evals.prompt: $DDev review this small function for naming and error handling. The cached code-style rule file is missing after I removed the global rule installation.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev and recognize code review as an operation that requires the cached code-style rule. It should run or ask for deweyou-cli agent update, re-check ~/.deweyou/agents/assets/rules/code-style.md, and stop the code review as blocked if the file remains missing. It should not require global rule installation and should not continue silently without the mandatory rule. Engineering-principles is not required unless the review expands into architecture-impacting work.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Recognized code review as requiring the cached code-style rule
Ran or requested deweyou-cli agent update before treating the missing cache file as final
Re-checked the exact cached rule path after refresh
Stopped the affected review if code-style remained missing
Did not require global rule installation
Did not load engineering-principles without an architecture-impacting operation

evals.prompt: $DDev help me implement a todo list.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, recognize an underspecified new user-facing feature, and load spec-driven-coding for feature alignment before editing product source. It should ask only material questions such as scope, persistence, completion or deletion behavior, and placement; produce a concise spec with acceptance criteria; mark the acceptance source as inferred and alignment as required when material choices remain; show the spec to the user; and wait for explicit confirmation without editing product source.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Classified the todo list as an underspecified new feature rather than a mechanical implementation task
Loaded or planned to load spec-driven-coding before product-source edits
Asked only material product questions and produced or planned a concise spec with acceptance criteria
Did not treat the request to implement as approval of agent-inferred requirements
Marked or planned alignment_required with inferred acceptance and unresolved decisions
Waited for explicit user confirmation and did not edit product source

evals.prompt: $DDev change the existing Add button label to New. Do not change its behavior.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, recognize a fully specified mechanical UI text edit, record that confirmation is not required because the behavior and boundary come directly from the user, and proceed through the focused implementation and verification path without inventing a feature spec or asking for unnecessary approval.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Classified the request as a mechanical edit with no unresolved product behavior
Recorded or planned confirmation_not_required with the user request as the acceptance source
Did not block on a new feature spec or unnecessary user confirmation
Kept the implementation limited to the label change and verification

evals.prompt: $DDev implement a todo list. You can choose reversible interaction details, but ask me before deciding persistence, permissions, or destructive behavior.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev and use spec-driven-coding before product-source edits. It may choose and state reversible low-risk interaction details because the user delegated them, but it must identify persistence, permissions, and destructive behavior as unresolved material decisions, present the partial concise spec, mark alignment as required, ask the user, and wait before editing product source.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Loaded or planned to load spec-driven-coding before product-source edits
Distinguished delegated reversible interaction details from material persistence, permission, and destructive decisions
Produced or planned a concise spec that exposes both chosen details and unresolved decisions
Marked or planned alignment_required and waited for explicit confirmation
Did not edit product source while material decisions remained unresolved

evals.prompt: $DDev this multi-step migration has a failed verification node and a changes-requested review. Record the evidence and resume only from the affected implementation boundary; do not build an automatic scheduler.
evals.expected_output: The run should trigger ddev, keep graph.md and evidence.md as the human recovery surface, use validated DDev protocol events for the node, evidence, failure, review, and recovery facts, include a reviewable restart_from hint for the affected implementation boundary, regenerate the single-session summary, and avoid implementing or pretending to use an automatic DAG scheduler or retry loop.
expectations: Triggered the ddev skill
Kept human-readable graph.md and evidence.md as the task and proof surface
Used or planned deweyou-cli dev record for structured node, evidence, failure, review, and recovery events
Linked the failure and review to evidence and a smallest-affected restart_from hint
Used or planned deweyou-cli dev summary to regenerate the branch-session summary
Treated restart_from as a reviewable hint rather than an automatic retry
Did not introduce or claim an automatic DAG scheduler
